data.title: AI language tutor startup Speak raises $78M at $1B valuation from OpenAI, others
data.description:
AI language tutor startup Speak has raised $78M in a new funding round, at a $1B valuation.

The Series C round was led by Accel with support from existing investors including the OpenAI Startup Fund, Khosla Ventures, and Y Combinator among others.

Speak said on Tuesday that the funding marked its second preempted raise this year, with the company's valuation doubling in the six months since its last round. The San Francisco-based startup has raised $162M to date.

"Speak has emerged as a standout player in consumer AI, demonstrating exceptional growth and market potential," said Ben Quazzo, partner at Accel.

Quazzo will join Speak's board.

Speak noted that this year it created over 25 million personalized lessons for its users, adapting to their pace and style of learning. Over the next year, Speak plans to become a dynamic learning experience powered by its underlying learning engine, the latest Large language models, or LLMs, and advancements in its speech technology.

Speak's Co-Founder and CEO Connor Zwick said that since the company's previous funding round, they have introduced an enterprise offering called Speak for Business to bring English learning to employers everywhere.

Generative AI services have become the talk of the town since the launch of Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI's ChatGPT in 2022. Globally, companies have launched their own LLMs, which can provide services such as content, image, video, and voice generation, to name a few.

data.title: Argentina ETF up 66%, surpassing all country ETFs by far
data.description:
Argentina ETF (NYSEARCA:ARGT) is by far the best performing country ETF in 2024 year-to-date.

Up 66%, it has surpassed the U.S. (SPY), China (MCHI), and any other country ETF.

Bespoke Investment Group posted in X.com that “investors seem to like the job [President Javier Milei] has done with the country.”

data.title: Trinseo signs agreement to extend debt maturities; shares up
data.description:
• Trinseo (NYSE:TSE) and its subsidiaries have signed a transaction support agreement with holders and lenders of its senior notes and term loans.
  • The transactions contemplated by the agreement include refinancing $115M of 2025 senior notes with $115M in 2028 refinance term loans, on similar terms to the September 2023 credit facility.
  • The company will secure a new $300M revolving credit facility, maturing in February 2028, to replace the existing facility due in May 2026.
  • The transaction will also include exchanging at least $330M of 2029 senior notes for new 2029 second lien senior secured notes at a discount to par, capturing at least $49M in discount value.
  • Upon consummation of the transactions contemplated by the agreement, the company will have no debt scheduled to mature until 2028.
  • The company plans to launch an exchange offer, offering holders of its 5.125% senior notes due 2029 the chance to exchange them for new 7.625% second lien senior secured notes due 2029, at a discount to the existing notes' par value.
  • The transactions, including the exchange offer, are expected to close in January 2025.
  • TSE shares were up 25.6% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CervoMed stock crashes 77% on failed study for dementia drug
data.description:
• Shares of CervoMed (NASDAQ:CRVO) plunged 77% Tuesday after the company said a Phase 2b study of its drug candidate neflamapimod for the treatment of dementia with Lewy bodies failed to meet its primary or key secondary endpoints.
  • CervoMed (CRVO) said the full data set from the study is expected to be available in January, with data from the initial 16 weeks of the open label extension portion of the study available in late Q2 2025, according to a statement.
  • Shares of CervoMed (CRVO) gained last month on news the drug had been granted Orphan Drug Designation by the FDA for frontotemporal dementia.

data.title: Hydro One subsidiary prices offering of $750M medium term notes
data.description:
• Hydro One Limited (TSX:H:CA) announced that its subsidiary, Hydro One, has priced a $750M medium term notes offering, including $375M of 4.46% notes due 2053 (Series 55) and $375M of 4.25% notes due 2035 (Series 60).
  • The additional Series 55 Notes will be issued at a price of $101.560 per $100.00 principal amount plus accrued interest, for a re-opening yield of 4.362%, and the additional Series 60 Notes will be issued at a price of $102.522 per $100.00 principal amount plus accrued interest, for a re-opening yield of 3.938%.
  • The net proceeds from the issuance of the additional notes will be ~$762M.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • Upon completion of the offering, there will be $675M aggregate principal amount of Series 55 notes outstanding and $1.075B aggregate principal amount of Series 60 Notes outstanding.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Airlines rallies after Bernstein turns bullish on the airline stock
data.description:
American Airlines Group (NASDAQ:AAL) flew higher on Tuesday after Bernstein upgraded the airline stock to an Outperform rating after having it slotted at Market Perform.

Analyst David Vernon pointed to positive pricing and revenue trends across the industry that also showed up in American Airlines' (AAL) recent guidance. "American Airlines [is] closer to a have than a have-not," summarized Vernon on the stock's upside. "AAL’s ability to deleverage thanks to improving industry backdrop [and] a new exclusive co-branded credit card deal enhances the company outlook," he added.

Looking ahead, Vernon said the benefits of such a sizable, consistent cash flow changes the story for an airline critiqued for its high leverage. In particular, AAL’s ability to more easily pay down its debts enhances the overall outlook, per Vernon.

Bernstein hiked its price target on American Airlines (AAL) to $24 from $14.

Shares of American Airlines (AAL) jumped 4.1% in the first half hour of trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Vestis gains amid report of takeover interest from buyout shops
data.description:
Vestis (NYSE:VSTS) rose 0.6% after a report that some private equity shops are evaluation a potential takeover of the uniform supplier.

PE firms Advent International and Apollo Global (APO) are exploring a potential acquisition of Vestis (VSTS), according to a Reuters report on Tuesday. Buyout shop Clayton Dubilier & Rice has also expressed interest in a potential offer for Vestis (VSTS).

The potential buyers have submitted initial offers in recent weeks, according to the report.

News of the PE offers comes after French competitor Elis in early October said it ended discussions with Vestis (VSTS) and US peer UniFirst (UNF) over possible takeover offers.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ALK, ORCL, AI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures exhibited mixed performance on Tuesday as investors eagerly awaited the U.S. consumer price index report, scheduled for release on Wednesday. This report could play a key role in shaping the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rates during its December 17-18 meeting.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • C3.ai (AI) shares gained over 7% after the company raised its full-year guidance following stronger-than-expected FQ2 results, driven by robust artificial intelligence-related spending. The company now expects FY2025 revenue to range from $378M to $398M, up from the prior forecast of $370M to $395M, surpassing the consensus of $382.6M. For Q3, C3.ai anticipates revenue between $95.5M and $100.5M, with the midpoint of $98M slightly exceeding the consensus of $97.5M.
  • Vail Resorts (MTN) shares rose nearly 5% after surpassing consensus expectations in its FQ1 results and raising its FY2025 outlook. The company increased its net income guidance for the fiscal year ending July 31, 2025, to a range of $240M to $316M, up from the prior forecast of $224M to $300M, largely driven by the sale of real estate. This revised guidance assumes stable economic conditions, normal weather for the 2024/2025 North American and European ski seasons, the 2025 Australian ski season, and consistent foreign exchange rates.
  • Shares of Alaska Air Group (ALK) surged 12% after the company raised its Q4 earnings outlook to $0.40-$0.50 per share, up from the previous guidance of $0.20-$0.40. The company attributed the improvement to stronger-than-expected close-in bookings in October and November, as well as better-than-anticipated November revenue despite shifts in Thanksgiving travel patterns. During its investor day in New York, Alaska Air outlined plans to grow profits by $1B through 2027, leveraging strong demand for high-end travel. The company also projected pretax margins of 11%-13% and earnings per share exceeding $10 by 2027. In October, Alaska estimated 2024 earnings between $3.50 and $4.50 per share, including Hawaiian Airlines' results.Biggest stock losers
  • Oracle (ORCL) shares slumped more than 8% after reporting lower-than-expected Q2 results. For the current quarter, the IT giant forecasts revenue growth of 7% to 9%, below the consensus estimate of 10.33%, and expects adjusted earnings of $1.50 to $1.54 per share, matching the consensus of $1.50.
  • MongoDB (MDB) shares plunged 10% despite delivering better-than-expected FQ3 results and a positive outlook. The decline followed the announcement that Michael Gordon, the company’s CFO and COO for nearly a decade, will retire on January 31, 2025.

data.title: S&P 500 sector weekly ETF flows: inflow resumes, bitcoin gains on higher inflows
data.description:
Investors were overall net purchasers of 11 S&P 500 sector ETFs, including SPDR S&P 500 Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) after redeeming funds in the prior week.

The past week, ending December 06, saw net inflows of $7.08B, with SPY adding $6.17B to its AUM, reversing its outflow of $1.37B in the earlier week.

Bitcoin ETF Flows iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) saw an inflow of $908M over the week, higher than the $781M inflow in the prior week. Bitcoin finally surpassed the $100K level after languishing below that milestone for nearly a month.

Index performance

At the close of the week, U.S. benchmark indexes marked two big milestones: the S&P 500 (SP500) surged past 6,100 points in a historic first, and the benchmark index has now smashed past the year-end targets of most of the brokerages on the Street.

For the week, the S&P climbed +1.0%, while the Nasdaq Composite added +3.3%. The Dow slipped 0.6%.

Last week’s inflows/ outflows:

The investing trend has been similar for the past 2 weeks with financials, consumer staples, industrial, materials, utilities, real estate and communication services reportedly led the buying activity, except for consumer discretionary fund which saw an outflow of $383.74M compared to inflow of $275.94M in the prior week.

Financials recorded the highest inflow among the sectors, attracting $842.1M in inflows, followed by consumer staples which had inflows of $543M in the last week.

Breakdown of S&P 500 sector fund flows (highest to lowest):

Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF): $842.16M.
Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP): $543M.
Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI): $248.4M.
Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLB): $184.3M.
Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU): $81.8M.
Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE): $36M.
Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC): $25M.
Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV): -$67M.
Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK): -$214.6M.
Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE): -$382.3M.
Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY): -$383.7M.
Commodities and Bitcoin ETF fund flows:
U.S. Oil Fund, LP ETF (USO): $28.4M.
Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD): $(562.2M).
Silver iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV): $29.6M.
Bitcoin ETF Flows iShares Bitcoin Trust Registered (IBIT): $908.01M.
ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI): $3.89M
